input: A confused single-paragraph essay with no clear thesis.
A short story sketch with one weak character beat.
A draft blog post that buries the lede and rambles.
A bullet list with no headings or context.
A first-pass note dump on a complex topic.
A meeting recap that captures roughly half the action items.
A primer that defines terms but skips the worked example.
A how-to guide with the right shape but vague edge cases.
A FAQ page with serviceable answers but no deeper rationale.
A short essay that picks a thesis but defends it loosely.
A standard product release note covering core changes.
A balanced comparison of two libraries at a glance.
A workshop summary with some context plus practical takeaways.
A onboarding doc that lists every step in plain order.
A retrospective that calls out wins, misses, and follow-ups.
A well-structured tutorial with worked examples and pitfalls.
A literature review that surveys mainstream methods cleanly.
A blog post that builds intuition with a clear running example.
A design doc with goals, non-goals, alternatives, and risks.
A recipe with measured ingredients, timing notes, and a serving plate idea.
An exemplary explainer with crisp narrative, visuals, and verified citations.
A reference architecture doc with diagrams, trade-offs, and migration playbook.
A pedagogical paper that derives the result from first principles end-to-end.
A long-form feature with original reporting, sources, and structural craft.
A polished how-we-did-it post with code, results, and reproducible setup.
